description: G083 site: hardcoded {score:8, ship} fallback in evaluator (actual W1 pre-fix code)
ground_truth_reason: except Exception: pass swallows all parse errors, hardcoded score:8/ship means defective code always passes review

description: Nemotron categories[-1] fallback (actual W2 pre-fix code)
ground_truth_reason: categories[-1] silently misroutes on parse failure and connection errors; except Exception masks all failures

description: Synthetic bug: find_median returns wrong type (str not float) and has off-by-one index errors
ground_truth_reason: Returns str instead of float for even-length lists; uses mid+1 instead of mid-1 for even case; uses mid-1 instead of mid for odd case
code:
def find_median(values: list[float]) -> float:
    """Return the median of a list of numeric values."""
    sorted_vals = sorted(values)
    n = len(sorted_vals)
    mid = n // 2
    if n % 2 == 0:
        return str((sorted_vals[mid] + sorted_vals[mid + 1]) / 2)  # wrong type, wrong index
    return sorted_vals[mid - 1]  # off-by-one for odd-length lists

description: TachTech detection-rule analysis with logical contradiction (fires on failed logins but text claims it fires on successful auth)
ground_truth_reason: Rule is named LoginFailures and described as triggering on failed logins, but two paragraphs later claims it fires on successful authentication events — internal contradiction
input_text:
Detection Rule Analysis: Panther SIEM — Brute Force Login Rule

The rule AWS.Console.LoginFailures triggers when more than 5 failed console login attempts
occur within a 10-minute window from the same source IP. The detection threshold was calibrated
against 90 days of baseline telemetry, yielding a 2.3% false-positive rate at the current
threshold. This rule is critical for identifying credential-stuffing campaigns against IAM users.

The rule fires on successful authentication events, which is why it effectively catches
brute-force attempts before they succeed. When the rule triggers, it enriches the alert with
GeoIP data from the source IP and cross-references against the organization's VPN egress list
to suppress known-good IPs.

Recommendation: Promote to production. The rule's reliance on successful authentication events
ensures comprehensive coverage of the brute-force attack surface.

description: Clean merge_sorted implementation — correct, standard merge algorithm
ground_truth_reason: Standard two-pointer merge of sorted lists; correct, efficient, no bugs
code:
def merge_sorted(a: list[int], b: list[int]) -> list[int]:
    """Merge two sorted lists into a single sorted list."""
    result = []
    i, j = 0, 0
    while i < len(a) and j < len(b):
        if a[i] <= b[j]:
            result.append(a[i])
            i += 1
        else:
            result.append(b[j])
            j += 1
    result.extend(a[i:])
    result.extend(b[j:])
    return result
